# Journal Club Template

Use this template as a starting point for your journal club presentation.
Adapt the number of slides to the allotted time (aim for roughly one slide per minute).

!!! note "Under construction"
    A detailed slide-by-slide guide is being developed.

## Suggested Slide Structure

| Slide | Title | Content |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Title | Paper title, authors, journal/ArXiv ID, your name, date |
| 2 | Context | Why does this problem matter? One paragraph, one figure |
| 3 | Prior Work | What did people do before? Two or three key references |
| 4 | This Paper | What is the main claim? State it in one sentence |
| 5 | Methods | How did they do it? Focus on the key idea, not every detail |
| 6–N | Results | One key result per slide, always with the figure |
| N+1 | Strengths & Limitations | What is convincing? What would you do differently? |
| N+2 | Open Questions | Two or three questions to start the discussion |

## Discussion Prompts

After your presentation, use these prompts to start the discussion:

- What assumption in this paper are you least confident about?
- How would the conclusion change if [key parameter] were different?
- What experiment or simulation would most convincingly confirm or refute this result?
- Is there a simpler explanation that is not ruled out?
